content: Form I is used for giving prior intimation or seeking previous sanction under Rule 18(2) of the CCS (Conduct) Rules, 1964 for acquisition or disposal of immovable property.
The applicant must provide property details including location, description, ownership status, nature of interest, ownership share, market value, and source of finance.
The applicant must disclose the name and address of the party involved in the transaction, relationship with the party, official dealings, and method of arranging the transaction.
The applicant must declare that the information furnished is true and either seek permission or provide prior intimation for acquisition or disposal of immovable property.
Applications requiring previous sanction must be submitted at least 30 days before the proposed date of transaction.
Form II is used for giving prior intimation or seeking previous sanction under Rule 18(3) of the CCS (Conduct) Rules, 1964 for acquisition or disposal of movable property.
The applicant must provide details of movable property such as vehicles, jewellery, appliances, loans, insurance policies, ownership status, transaction value, and source of finance.
The applicant must disclose details of the party involved in the transaction, relationship with the party, official dealings, and method of arranging the transaction.
The applicant must certify the correctness of the information and either seek sanction or provide intimation regarding the transaction.
Where previous sanction is required, the application should be submitted at least 30 days before the proposed transaction date.
